```
import { chromium } from 'playwright';
import { mkdir, readFile, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
import path from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

const __dirname = fileURLToPath(new URL('.', import.meta.url));
const PROJECT_ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '..');

function parseArgs(argv) {
  const args = {};
  for (const arg of argv) {
    if (!arg.startsWith('--')) continue;
    const [key, value = 'true'] = arg.slice(2).split('=');
    args[key] = value;
  }
  return args;
}

function stamp() {
  return new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
}

const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
const url = args.url || 'http://127.0.0.1:5173';
const width = Number(args.width || 1600);
const height = Math.round(width / (16 / 9));
const quality = Number(args.quality || 88);
const scale = Number(args.scale || 1);
const renderer = args.renderer || 'screenshot';
const outputDir = path.resolve(PROJECT_ROOT, args.out || `anki_build/slides_${stamp()}`);
const chromePath = args.chrome || '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome';

async function waitForSlides(page) {
  await page.locator('.slide').first().waitFor({ timeout: 15000 });
  await page.evaluate(() => document.fonts?.ready);
  await page.waitForTimeout(1200);
}

async function main() {
  const contentPath = path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, 'content.json');
  const content = JSON.parse(await readFile(contentPath, 'utf8'));
  const totalSlides = content.slides.length;

  await mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true });

  let browser;
  try {
    browser = await chromium.launch();
  } catch (error) {
    if (!error.message.includes("Executable doesn't exist")) throw error;
    browser = await chromium.launch({ executablePath: chromePath });
  }
  const page = await browser.newPage({
    viewport: { width, height },
    deviceScaleFactor: scale,
  });

  await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
  await page.evaluate(() => {
    sessionStorage.setItem('deckPage', '0');
    location.reload();
  });
  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
  await waitForSlides(page);

  let captured = 0;
  while (captured < totalSlides) {
    const slides = page.locator('.slide-wrapper .slide');
    const visibleCount = await slides.count();

    for (let i = 0; i < visibleCount && captured < totalSlides; i += 1) {
      const slideNumber = captured + 1;
      const slide = content.slides[captured];
      const filename = `slide-${String(slideNumber).padStart(2, '0')}-${slide.type}.jpg`;
      const filepath = path.join(outputDir, filename);

      if (renderer === 'html2canvas') {
        const dataUrl = await slides.nth(i).evaluate(
          async (el, { captureScale, captureQuality }) => {
            const { default: html2canvas } = await import('/node_modules/.vite/deps/html2canvas.js');
            const canvas = await html2canvas(el, {
              scale: captureScale,
              useCORS: true,
              logging: false,
              backgroundColor: null,
            });
            return canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', captureQuality / 100);
          },
          { captureScale: scale, captureQuality: quality },
        );
        await writeFile(filepath, Buffer.from(dataUrl.split(',')[1], 'base64'));
      } else {
        await slides.nth(i).screenshot({
          path: filepath,
          type: 'jpeg',
          quality,
          animations: 'disabled',
        });
      }

      console.log(`Exported ${filename}`);
      captured += 1;
    }

    if (captured >= totalSlides) break;

    const infoBefore = await page.locator('#pagination-top .pg-info').textContent();
    await page.locator('#pagination-top .pg-next').click();
    await page.waitForFunction(
      previous => document.querySelector('#pagination-top .pg-info')?.textContent !== previous,
      infoBefore,
    );
    await waitForSlides(page);
  }

  await browser.close();
  console.log(`\nExported ${captured} current slides to ${outputDir}`);
}

main().catch(error => {
  console.error(error);
  process.exit(1);
});
```
</details></tool-use>

```
---
name: "pdf"
description: "Use when tasks involve reading, creating, or reviewing PDF files where rendering and layout matter; prefer visual checks by rendering pages (Poppler) and use Python tools such as `reportlab`, `pdfplumber`, and `pypdf` for generation and extraction."
---


# PDF Skill

## When to use
- Read or review PDF content where layout and visuals matter.
- Create PDFs programmatically with reliable formatting.
- Validate final rendering before delivery.

## Workflow
1. Prefer visual review: render PDF pages to PNGs and inspect them.
   - Use `pdftoppm` if available.
   - If unavailable, install Poppler or ask the user to review the output locally.
2. Use `reportlab` to generate PDFs when creating new documents.
3. Use `pdfplumber` (or `pypdf`) for text extraction and quick checks; do not rely on it for layout fidelity.
4. After each meaningful update, re-render pages and verify alignment, spacing, and legibility.

## Temp and output conventions
- Use `tmp/pdfs/` for intermediate files; delete when done.
- Write final artifacts under `output/pdf/` when working in this repo.
- Keep filenames stable and descriptive.

## Dependencies (install if missing)
Prefer `uv` for dependency management.

Python packages:
```
uv pip install reportlab pdfplumber pypdf
```
If `uv` is unavailable:
```
python3 -m pip install reportlab pdfplumber pypdf
```
System tools (for rendering):
```
# macOS (Homebrew)
brew install poppler

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt-get install -y poppler-utils
```

If installation isn't possible in this environment, tell the user which dependency is missing and how to install it locally.

## Environment
No required environment variables.

## Rendering command
```
pdftoppm -png $INPUT_PDF $OUTPUT_PREFIX
```

## Quality expectations
- Maintain polished visual design: consistent typography, spacing, margins, and section hierarchy.
- Avoid rendering issues: clipped text, overlapping elements, broken tables, black squares, or unreadable glyphs.
- Charts, tables, and images must be sharp, aligned, and clearly labeled.
- Use ASCII hyphens only. Avoid U+2011 (non-breaking hyphen) and other Unicode dashes.
- Citations and references must be human-readable; never leave tool tokens or placeholder strings.

## Final checks
- Do not deliver until the latest PNG inspection shows zero visual or formatting defects.
- Confirm headers/footers, page numbering, and section transitions look polished.
- Keep intermediate files organized or remove them after final approval.
```

```bash
from PIL import Image, ImageChops, ImageStat
from pathlib import Path
clean=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_clean_low')
marked=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_marked_low')
for i in range(1,89):
    c=Image.open(clean/f'page-{i:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    m=Image.open(marked/f'page-{i:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    d=ImageChops.difference(c,m)
    bbox=d.getbbox()
    if not bbox:
        continue
    # count pixels above a small threshold
    gray=d.convert('L')
    mask=gray.point(lambda p: 255 if p>20 else 0)
    pix=sum(1 for p in mask.getdata() if p)
    if pix>50:
        print(f'page {i:03d}: diff_pixels={pix} bbox={bbox}')
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.4 2026-04-12 15:35:02Z)**_

_**Agent (gpt-5.4 2026-04-12 15:35:14Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from PIL import Image, ImageChops, ImageDraw, ImageFont
from pathlib import Path
pages=[1,2,4,6,10,12,14,16,24,50,51,52,58,64,66,68,74,82,88]
clean=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_clean_hi')
marked=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_marked_hi')
out=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_crops')
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
try:
    font=ImageFont.truetype('/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial.ttf', 28)
except Exception:
    font=ImageFont.load_default()
thumbs=[]
for p in pages:
    c=Image.open(clean/f'page-{p:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    m=Image.open(marked/f'page-{p:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    d=ImageChops.difference(c,m).convert('L')
    mask=d.point(lambda x: 255 if x>18 else 0)
    bbox=mask.getbbox()
    if not bbox:
        continue
    x0,y0,x1,y1=bbox
    pad=120
    x0=max(0,x0-pad); y0=max(0,y0-pad); x1=min(m.width,x1+pad); y1=min(m.height,y1+pad)
    crop=m.crop((x0,y0,x1,y1))
    label_h=46
    canvas=Image.new('RGB',(crop.width,crop.height+label_h),'white')
    draw=ImageDraw.Draw(canvas)
    draw.text((12,8),f'PDF page {p}',fill='black',font=font)
    canvas.paste(crop,(0,label_h))
    path=out/f'crop-page-{p:03d}.png'
    canvas.save(path)
    # make thumbnail for contact sheet, max width 620
    maxw=620
    scale=min(1,maxw/canvas.width)
    thumb=canvas.resize((int(canvas.width*scale), int(canvas.height*scale)))
    thumbs.append((p,thumb))
# contact sheets in groups of 6
for group_idx in range(0,len(thumbs),6):
    group=thumbs[group_idx:group_idx+6]
    w=max(t.width for _,t in group)
    h=sum(t.height for _,t in group)+20*(len(group)-1)
    sheet=Image.new('RGB',(w,h),'white')
    y=0
    for _,t in group:
        sheet.paste(t,(0,y))
        y+=t.height+20
    sheet.save(out/f'contact-{group_idx//6+1}.png')
print(out)
PY
```

```
Command: /bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from PIL import Image, ImageChops, ImageDraw, ImageFont
from pathlib import Path
pages=[1,2,4,6,10,12,14,16,24,50,51,52,58,64,66,68,74,82,88]
clean=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_clean_hi')
marked=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_marked_hi')
out=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_crops')
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
try:
    font=ImageFont.truetype('/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial.ttf', 28)
except Exception:
    font=ImageFont.load_default()
thumbs=[]
for p in pages:
    c=Image.open(clean/f'page-{p:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    m=Image.open(marked/f'page-{p:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    d=ImageChops.difference(c,m).convert('L')
    mask=d.point(lambda x: 255 if x>18 else 0)
    bbox=mask.getbbox()
    if not bbox:
        continue
    x0,y0,x1,y1=bbox
    pad=120
    x0=max(0,x0-pad); y0=max(0,y0-pad); x1=min(m.width,x1+pad); y1=min(m.height,y1+pad)
    crop=m.crop((x0,y0,x1,y1))
    label_h=46
    canvas=Image.new('RGB',(crop.width,crop.height+label_h),'white')
    draw=ImageDraw.Draw(canvas)
    draw.text((12,8),f'PDF page {p}',fill='black',font=font)
    canvas.paste(crop,(0,label_h))
    path=out/f'crop-page-{p:03d}.png'
    canvas.save(path)
    # make thumbnail for contact sheet, max width 620
    maxw=620
    scale=min(1,maxw/canvas.width)
    thumb=canvas.resize((int(canvas.width*scale), int(canvas.height*scale)))
    thumbs.append((p,thumb))
# contact sheets in groups of 6
for group_idx in range(0,len(thumbs),6):
    group=thumbs[group_idx:group_idx+6]
    w=max(t.width for _,t in group)
    h=sum(t.height for _,t in group)+20*(len(group)-1)
    sheet=Image.new('RGB',(w,h),'white')
    y=0
    for _,t in group:
        sheet.paste(t,(0,y))
        y+=t.height+20
    sheet.save(out/f'contact-{group_idx//6+1}.png')
print(out)
```

```
from PIL import Image, ImageChops, ImageDraw, ImageFont
from pathlib import Path
pages=[1,2,4,6,10,12,14,16,24,50,51,52,58,64,66,68,74,82,88]
clean=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_clean_hi')
marked=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_marked_hi')
out=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_crops')
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
try:
    font=ImageFont.truetype('/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial.ttf', 28)
except Exception:
    font=ImageFont.load_default()
thumbs=[]
for p in pages:
    c=Image.open(clean/f'page-{p:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    m=Image.open(marked/f'page-{p:03d}.png').convert('RGB')
    d=ImageChops.difference(c,m).convert('L')
    mask=d.point(lambda x: 255 if x>18 else 0)
    bbox=mask.getbbox()
    if not bbox:
        continue
    x0,y0,x1,y1=bbox
    pad=120
    x0=max(0,x0-pad); y0=max(0,y0-pad); x1=min(m.width,x1+pad); y1=min(m.height,y1+pad)
    crop=m.crop((x0,y0,x1,y1))
    label_h=46
    canvas=Image.new('RGB',(crop.width,crop.height+label_h),'white')
    draw=ImageDraw.Draw(canvas)
    draw.text((12,8),f'PDF page {p}',fill='black',font=font)
    canvas.paste(crop,(0,label_h))
    path=out/f'crop-page-{p:03d}.png'
    canvas.save(path)
    # make thumbnail for contact sheet, max width 620
    maxw=620
    scale=min(1,maxw/canvas.width)
    thumb=canvas.resize((int(canvas.width*scale), int(canvas.height*scale)))
    thumbs.append((p,thumb))
# contact sheets in groups of 6
for group_idx in range(0,len(thumbs),6):
    group=thumbs[group_idx:group_idx+6]
    w=max(t.width for _,t in group)
    h=sum(t.height for _,t in group)+20*(len(group)-1)
    sheet=Image.new('RGB',(w,h),'white')
    y=0
    for _,t in group:
        sheet.paste(t,(0,y))
        y+=t.height+20
    sheet.save(out/f'contact-{group_idx//6+1}.png')
print(out)
PY"
```

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from PIL import Image, ImageFilter
from pathlib import Path
src=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_ink')
out=Path('/tmp/pdfs/teesside_ink_zoom')
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
for p in [1,2,4,16,24,50,52,58,64,66,68,74,82,88]:
    im=Image.open(src/f'ink-page-{p:03d}.png').convert('L')
    # remove top label region where possible
    if im.height > 90:
        im=im.crop((0,70,im.width,im.height))
    # crop to content bbox
    inv=im.point(lambda x: 255-x)
    bbox=inv.point(lambda x: 255 if x>20 else 0).getbbox()
    if bbox:
        x0,y0,x1,y1=bbox
        pad=30
        im=im.crop((max(0,x0-pad),max(0,y0-pad),min(im.width,x1+pad),min(im.height,y1+pad)))
    im=im.resize((im.width*3, im.height*3))
    im.save(out/f'zoom-ink-page-{p:03d}.png')
print(out)
PY
```

```
      "Repurposed post-boom hardware: As hardware depreciates and supply loosens, some compute may become affordable for shared sector hosting or bounded institutional workloads.",
      "Shared smaller models: Open-weight and smaller models are increasingly good enough for retrieval, classification, transcription, analysis and support tasks common in universities.",
      "Interdisciplinary design: Computer science can build the systems, but education and social science are needed to define the tasks, evaluate the harms and shape the institutional fit."
    ]
  },
  "lenses": [
    "transparency",
    "distortion",
    "extraction"
  ]
}
```

```
/**
 * Layout template functions.
 * Each returns an HTML string for the slide's inner content.
 * Layouts reference CSS custom properties — never hard-coded colours/fonts.
 */

const LENS_META = {
  transparency: { short: 'T', label: 'Transparency' },
  distortion: { short: 'D', label: 'Distortion' },
  extraction: { short: 'E', label: 'Extraction' },
};

function renderLensMarkers(slide) {
  const hasLenses = Array.isArray(slide.lenses) && slide.lenses.length > 0;

  const markers = hasLenses
    ? slide.lenses
        .filter(lens => LENS_META[lens])
        .map((lens) => {
          const meta = LENS_META[lens];
          return `<span class="lens-marker lens-marker--${lens} lens-marker--droplet" title="${meta.label}"><canvas class="lens-droplet-canvas" data-lens="${lens}"></canvas><span class="lens-droplet-letter">${meta.short}</span></span>`;
        })
        .join('')
    : '';

  return `<div class="lens-marker-group" aria-label="Applied lenses">${markers}</div>`;
}

// ─── COVER LAYOUTS ──────────────────────────────

export function coverBoldFull(slide) {
  return `
    <div class="slide-inner cover-bold-full">
      <div class="cover-title">${slide.title}</div>
      ${slide.subtitle ? `<div class="cover-subtitle">${slide.subtitle}</div>` : ''}
      <div class="cover-bottom">
        <div class="cover-author">${slide.author || ''}</div>
        <div class="cover-date-pill">${slide.date || ''}</div>
      </div>
      ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
    </div>
  `;
}

export function coverDarkMinimal(slide) {
  return `
    <div class="slide-inner cover-dark-minimal">
      <div class="cover-top">
        <div class="cover-badge">${slide.badge || ''}</div>
        <div class="cover-date-pill">${slide.date || ''}</div>
      </div>
      <div class="cover-title">${slide.title}</div>
      <div class="cover-subtitle">${slide.subtitle || ''}</div>
      ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
    </div>
  `;
}

// ─── SECTION DIVIDER LAYOUTS ────────────────────

export function dividerColorBurst(slide, page) {
  return `
    <div class="slide-inner divider-color-burst">
      <div class="divider-center">
        ${slide.section_number != null
          ? `<div class="divider-badge">Section ${slide.section_number}</div>`
          : ''}
        <div class="divider-title">${slide.title}</div>
      </div>
      <div class="divider-footer">
        <span class="divider-page">${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}</span>
        ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
      </div>
    </div>
  `;
}

export function dividerDarkCinematic(slide, page) {
  return `
    <div class="slide-inner divider-dark-cinematic">
      <div class="divider-top">
        ${slide.section_number != null
          ? `<div class="divider-badge">Section ${slide.section_number}</div>`
          : '<span></span>'}
        <div class="divider-page">${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}</div>
      </div>
      <div class="divider-title">${slide.title}</div>
      ${slide.subtitle ? `<div class="divider-subtitle">${slide.subtitle}</div>` : ''}
      ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
    </div>
  `;
}

// ─── CONTENT LAYOUTS ────────────────────────────

export function contentSplitCards(slide, page) {
  const colors = [
    'var(--color-accent-1)',
    'var(--color-accent-2)',
    'var(--color-accent-3)',
    'var(--color-accent-4)',
  ];

  const cards = (slide.points || []).map((point, i) => `
    <div class="evidence-card">
      <div class="card-icon" style="background: ${colors[i % colors.length]}"></div>
      <div class="card-text">
        <h3>${point.title}</h3>
      </div>
    </div>
  `).join('');

  return `
    <div class="slide-inner content-split-cards">
      <div class="content-nav">${slide.section || ''}</div>
      <div class="content-body-area">
        <div class="content-left">
          <h2>${slide.heading}</h2>
          ${slide.body ? `<p>${slide.body}</p>` : ''}
        </div>
        <div class="content-right">
          ${cards}
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="content-footer">
        <span class="page-number">${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}</span>
        ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
      </div>
    </div>
  `;
}

export function contentWideVisual(slide, page) {
  const colors = [
    'var(--color-accent-1)',
    'var(--color-accent-2)',
    'var(--color-accent-3)',
    'var(--color-accent-4)',
  ];

  const cards = (slide.points || []).map((point, i) => `
    <div class="visual-card">
      <div class="card-accent" style="background: ${colors[i % colors.length]}"></div>
      <h3>${point.title}</h3>
      <p>${point.description}</p>
    </div>
  `).join('');
```

```
/* ============================================
   BASE STYLES — slide container + reset
   ============================================ */

*, *::before, *::after {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

body {
  background: #1a1a1a;
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  padding: 2rem;
}

/* --- Controls panel --- */
#controls {
  max-width: 1200px;
  margin: 0 auto 2rem;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem;
  background: #2a2a2a;
  border-radius: 0.75rem;
  color: #ccc;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  font-family: 'DM Mono', monospace;
  line-height: 1.6;
  position: sticky;
  top: 1rem;
  z-index: 100;
  box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
}

#controls .ctrl-header {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: center;
}

#controls .ctrl-body {
  margin-top: 1rem;
}

#controls h2 {
  color: #fff;
  font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif;
  font-size: 1rem;
  margin: 0;
}

#controls .ctrl-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}

#controls .ctrl-row--layouts {
  padding-top: 0.75rem;
  border-top: 1px solid #3a3a3a;
}

#controls .ctrl-group {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 0.3rem;
  flex: 1;
  min-width: 160px;
}

#controls .ctrl-label {
  color: #777;
  font-size: 0.65rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
}

#controls .ctrl-select {
  appearance: none;
  background: #1a1a1a;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  color: #5DD4E0;
  font-family: 'DM Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  padding: 0.45rem 0.6rem;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: border-color 0.15s;
}

#controls .ctrl-select:hover {
  border-color: #5DD4E0;
}

#controls .ctrl-select:focus {
  outline: none;
  border-color: #5DD4E0;
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(93, 212, 224, 0.2);
}

#controls .ctrl-range {
  appearance: none;
  width: 100%;
  height: 4px;
  background: #444;
  border-radius: 2px;
  outline: none;
  margin-top: 0.4rem;
  cursor: pointer;
}

#controls .ctrl-range::-webkit-slider-thumb {
  appearance: none;
  width: 14px;
  height: 14px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #5DD4E0;
  cursor: pointer;
}

#controls .ctrl-range::-moz-range-thumb {
  width: 14px;
  height: 14px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #5DD4E0;
  border: none;
  cursor: pointer;
}

#controls .ctrl-header-actions {
  display: flex;
  gap: 0.5rem;
  align-items: center;
}

/* Save button — must come after .ctrl-btn to override it */

#controls .ctrl-btn {
  appearance: none;
  background: #5DD4E0;
  color: #1a1a1a;
  border: none;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  padding: 0.5rem 1.2rem;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: opacity 0.15s;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn:hover {
  opacity: 0.85;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--save {
  background: #444;
  color: #999;
  cursor: default;
  opacity: 0.6;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--save:not(:disabled) {
  background: #5DD4E0;
  color: #1a1a1a;
  cursor: pointer;
  opacity: 1;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--export {
  background: transparent;
  border: 1px solid #5DD4E0;
  color: #5DD4E0;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  padding: 0.4rem 0.8rem;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--export:hover {
  background: rgba(93, 212, 224, 0.1);
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--export:disabled {
  opacity: 0.5;
  cursor: default;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--toggle {
  background: transparent;
  border: 1px solid #555;
  color: #999;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  padding: 0.4rem 0.8rem;
}

#controls .ctrl-btn--toggle:hover {
  border-color: #888;
  color: #ccc;
}

/* --- Pagination --- */
.pagination {

  font-size: 0.8rem;

  cursor: pointer;
  transition: border-color 0.15s, opacity 0.15s;
```

```
/* ============================================
   LAYOUT: cover-bold-full
   Inspired by: Monzo
   Full-bleed accent colour, massive centred title
   ============================================ */

.cover-bold-full {
  background: var(--color-accent-1);
  color: var(--color-text-on-dark);
  flex-direction: column;
  padding: var(--slide-padding);
}

.cover-bold-full .cover-title {
  font-family: var(--font-display);
  font-weight: var(--font-display-weight);
  font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 8cqi, var(--heading-size-xl));
  line-height: var(--heading-line-height);
  letter-spacing: var(--heading-spacing);
  text-transform: var(--heading-transform);
  margin-top: auto;  /* push title to vertical centre */
  word-break: break-word;
}

.cover-bold-full .cover-subtitle {
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.2cqi, var(--heading-size-md));
  font-weight: 400;
  margin-top: 0.8em;
  opacity: 0.85;
}

.cover-bold-full .cover-bottom {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: flex-end;
  margin-top: auto;  /* push bottom bar down */
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

  color: #666;

}

}

.slide-export-btn:disabled {

/* --- Slide container: 16:9 aspect ratio (overridable via --export-aspect) --- */
.slide {
  width: 100%;
  aspect-ratio: var(--export-aspect, 16 / 9);
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
  border-radius: 0.25rem;
  /* Shadow for visibility against dark page bg */
  box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);
}

.slide {
  container-type: inline-size;
}

.slide-inner {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  position: relative;
  display: flex;
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  font-weight: var(--font-body-weight);
  font-size: var(--body-size);
  line-height: var(--body-line-height);
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* Safety: all text containers can shrink in flex layouts */
.slide-inner * {
  min-height: 0;
  min-width: 0;
}

.lens-marker-group {
  position: absolute;
  right: var(--slide-padding);
  bottom: var(--slide-padding);
  left: auto;
```

```
// ─── STATEMENT LAYOUTS ──────────────────────────

export function statementCentered(slide) {
  const text = slide.text.replace(
    new RegExp(`(${escapeRegex(slide.highlight)})`, 'gi'),
    '<span class="highlight">$1</span>'
  );

  return `
    <div class="slide-inner statement-centered">
      <div class="statement-label">${slide.section || ''}</div>
      <div class="statement-text">${text}</div>
      ${slide.attribution ? `<div class="statement-attribution">${slide.attribution}</div>` : ''}
      ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
    </div>
  `;
}

export function statementEditorial(slide) {
  const text = slide.text.replace(
    new RegExp(`(${escapeRegex(slide.highlight)})`, 'gi'),
    '<span class="highlight">$1</span>'
  );

  return `
    <div class="slide-inner statement-editorial">
      <div class="statement-label">${slide.section || ''}</div>
      <div class="statement-text">${text}</div>
      ${slide.attribution ? `<div class="statement-attribution">${slide.attribution}</div>` : ''}
      ${renderLensMarkers(slide)}
    </div>
  `;
}

// ─── HELPERS ────────────────────────────────────

function escapeRegex(str) {
  if (!str) return '';
  return str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
}

// ─── LAYOUT REGISTRY ────────────────────────────

export const layoutRegistry = {
  // Covers
  'cover-bold-full':      coverBoldFull,
  'cover-dark-minimal':   coverDarkMinimal,

  // Dividers
  'divider-color-burst':     dividerColorBurst,
  'divider-dark-cinematic':  dividerDarkCinematic,

  // Content
  'content-split-cards':  contentSplitCards,
  'content-wide-visual':  contentWideVisual,

  // Data
  'data-big-numbers':     dataBigNumbers,
  'data-full-bleed':      dataFullBleed,

  // Statement
  'statement-centered':   statementCentered,
  'statement-editorial':  statementEditorial,
};
```

```
{
  "type": "content",
  "section": "A different strategy",
  "heading": "Ownership means owning some of the stack",
  "body": "Contracts matter, but universities do not really shape their AI future if every consequential layer remains external. Ownership in practice means deciding which parts of compute, models and oversight must stay institutionally or sector governed.",
  "points": [
    {
      "title": "Compute where it matters"
    },
    {
      "title": "Models for university tasks"
    },
    {
      "title": "Evaluation under our control"

  "notes": {
    "transition": "Make ownership concrete rather than abstract.",
    "points": [
      "Say that ownership is not only about procurement clauses; it is also about who controls key layers of the system.",
      "The claim is not that every university should own the entire stack.",
      "The claim is that some compute, some models and some evaluation capacity should be institutionally or sector governed where mission, resilience or contestability matter.",
      "Compute where it matters: Retain local or shared hosting for high-value functions where continuity, privacy or bargaining power are strategically important.",
      "Models for university tasks: Build, adapt or fine-tune models for bounded educational and administrative use cases rather than relying only on general-purpose systems.",
      "Evaluation under our control: Keep benchmark design, red-teaming and monitoring in hands that are accountable to educational values."
    ]

    "extraction"
```

```
Who Will Control the
AI Infrastructure
```

```
Frame the keynote as a shift from 'how should we use AI?' to 'who controls the AI we use?' | You will hear talks about pedagogy, assessment, and practice. | This talk is neither anti-AI nor anti-industry; it is about governance.

Dr Stuart Grey

--- slide 3: section-divider
Distributed Agents for Autonomous Spacecraft
Use this to show I have a machine learning / AI research background, not just a policy interest. | The point is that I understand AI systems as systems with architecture, coordination, and failure modes.
--- slide 5: statement
Transparency
Transparency asks: what can institutions actually see, inspect, explain, and contest? | Use it to frame provenance, auditability, model dependencies, data use, and institutional legibility.
--- slide 6: statement
Distortion
Distortion asks what gets bent when systems reward what is easy to capture, measure, or automate. | This will later connect to assessment design, pedagogy, workflow simplification, and institutional incentives.
--- slide 7: statement
Extraction
Extraction asks who captures value when educational activity becomes data, workflow, or dependency. | Later this will connect to data use, vendor leverage, upstream value capture, and platform power.
--- slide 8: statement
The AI argument in higher education is no longer mainly about whether the tools are impressive. It is about who sets the conditions of reliance.
Say that the argument is no longer mainly about whether the tools are impressive. | Say the deeper question is who sets the conditions of reliance as AI becomes normal infrastructure. | Use this as the bridge from the opening frame into the body of the talk.
--- slide 12: content
Universities need a viable system for AI governance, not just a policy
If governance is lagging behind use, the answer cannot be a thin guidance document. | The institution needs coordination, assurance, foresight and mission-level steering across the whole system. | Use this as the first practical move so the talk does not leave action until the end. | Coordinate the edge: Join up local experimentation so teaching, support, procurement and data decisions are not scattered. | Build assurance: Test tools, document risk and create routes for review and contestation before high-stakes use. | Steer by mission: Make senior governance articulate what kind of university the AI stack is supposed to serve.
--- slide 25: content
Pedagogy starts to bend around what the platform can measure
Platforms nudge teaching toward what they can count, standardise, and render visible. | Say that over time pedagogy can start adapting to platform logic rather than educational purpose. | This is a subtle but central claim: metrics and affordances become curricular pressure. | Standardisation pressure: Assessments and activities may drift toward formats that are easy to auto-score, summarise or monitor. | Surface metrics: Completion, engagement and usage are privileged because they are measurable, even when they are weak proxies for learning. | Platform visibility: Platforms and publishers can become the default route through which content, prompts and resources are surfaced.
--- slide 26: content
Educational data can be captured unless it is governed
Educational data does not only support services; it can be pulled into upstream value capture. | Explain behavioural exhaust, model improvement, upstream value capture and governance in plain institutional terms. | The room should hear that data governance is about political economy, not only compliance. | Behavioural exhaust: Prompts, drafts, clicks, revisions and case interactions all become telemetry. | Upstream capture: Value created from public educational activity can be captured upstream and turned into model advantage elsewhere. | Governed asset: Separate operational delivery, evaluation and model-improvement uses rather than letting them blur together.
--- slide 29: content
Path dependence becomes governance
```
